title: HTTP/2 Frames, Streams, HPACK, Flow Control, and Go Boundaries · Go Semantics Case: H2 Flow Priority Concurrency
summary: A team assumes HTTP/2 is compressed HTTP/1.1 text, shares one parser state across streams, ignores SETTINGS and flow control, indexes secrets into HPACK, and maps every stream failure to connection failure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the h2 flow priority concurrency increment in HTTP/2 connection, stream, frame, and compression model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Coordinate stream and connection flow control, frame size, concurrent streams, backpressure, fairness, reset, GOAWAY, and retry evidence.
Map pseudo-fields and regular fields, enforce ordering and connection-field rules, bound HPACK tables and header lists, and avoid indexing secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict H2 Flow Priority Concurrency · Theory H2 Flow Priority Concurrency
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9979 in a partner TLS endpoint negotiating HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 through ALPN. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine HTTP/2 frame, stream, flow-control, and header-list bounds; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the H2 Flow Priority Concurrency contract for the supplied data.
